mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive when the policy rate is falling and inflation is not accelerating faster than nominal rates.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from baseline in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling year-over-year, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals demand destruction and deteriorating earnings expectations, so when unemployment increases materially within a short window the S&P 500 tends to post negative 5-day forward returns as investors price recession risk immediately.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve together, consumer demand and housing activity confirm broad domestic expansion, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not beat either series alone in predictive power, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in the majority of periods with simultaneous month-over-month gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates stronger real output and tighter capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when industrial production is above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO episodes fail to show higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the return differential is negative over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy becomes more accommodative and nominal liquidity conditions support equities, creating positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns until inflation momentum becomes broad-based.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate and rising-inflation regime does not yield positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not superior to either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent payroll gains in PAYEMS indicate sustained income growth and consumer spending power, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month as analysts raise revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll streaks do not deliver above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is below 55% across all triggered occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS captures weakening aggregate demand that should depress corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns during slowdown phases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decelerate versus prior period
failure_condition: Reject if dual demand deceleration fails to outperform single-series signals in predicting negative returns, or if conditional forward returns are not below zero in a majority of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a rebound in HOUST together with rising INDPRO signals that real activity is normalizing, and equities should deliver positive 60-day forward returns as supply constraints ease and cyclical earnings recover.
failure_condition: Reject if recovery episodes with joint HOUST and INDPRO improvement do not show positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms an unconditional benchmark over any 3-year window.
